description: Worker-scoped managed browser pool that reuses a bounded Chromium CDP endpoint for browser-use invocations.

userStories.title: Add browser pool state model and lock
userStories.description: As a tooling engineer, I want a persisted worker-scoped browser pool state model so that browser-use invocations share one authority for pid, CDP port, leases, and health.
acceptanceCriteria: Add browser pool state types with worker id, pool id, max browser processes, pid, process group id when available, CDP port, CDP URL, user-data dir, active lease id, owner, last-used time, idle expiry, state, and last error
Add lock acquisition with bounded timeout for acquire/release/reap state mutations
Malformed or missing pool state fails safely or initializes an empty pool according to command context
Focused unit tests cover lock success, lock timeout, missing state, malformed state, and state write/read round trips
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented src/tools/browser-pool.ts with worker-scoped state types, path helpers, safe missing/malformed state loading, atomic write/read round trips, bounded file lock acquisition, and acquire/release/reap mutation wrapper. Added test/browser-pool-state.test.mjs. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused browser-pool tests passed in Docker worker; npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Implement managed CDP health and reuse decisions
userStories.description: As an agent, I want Pibo to reuse a healthy managed Chromium browser so that repeated UI checks do not start new browser main processes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a CDP health check that validates the recorded CDP URL through a bounded request such as /json/version
Acquire returns the existing managed CDP URL when the recorded browser is healthy
Acquire marks the recorded browser stale when the pid is dead, CDP is unreachable, or state identity is invalid
Acquire starts at most one replacement browser under lock when replacement is required
Focused tests cover healthy reuse, dead pid, unreachable CDP, malformed CDP response, and replacement start decision
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented managed browser-pool CDP health and acquire decisions in src/tools/browser-pool.ts: bounded /json/version health checks, healthy recorded CDP reuse, stale/dirty marking for dead pid, unreachable CDP, malformed/identity-invalid state, and locked replacement start. Added focused tests in test/browser-pool-state.test.mjs. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused browser-pool tests passed in Docker worker; npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Route browser-use wrapper through managed pool acquire
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I want normal browser-use commands in a compute worker to attach to Pibo's managed browser so that prompts do not need custom cleanup instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: Update the browser-use wrapper path so compute-worker browser-use invocations acquire a managed browser-pool lease before running browser-use
Pass the managed CDP URL to browser-use through the supported environment variable or argument path used by the wrapper
Do not silently fall back to starting an unmanaged browser when managed-pool acquire fails; report a clear error or documented fallback state
Real-path CLI or wrapper test verifies that the wrapper emits/uses a managed CDP URL for a compute-worker-like environment
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented managed browser-pool acquire in the generated browser-use wrapper: default browser-starting commands now acquire a worker/pool-scoped lock, reuse healthy recorded CDP state, start and record one managed Chrome/Chromium process when needed, pass the managed CDP URL through --cdp-url, and fail clearly instead of falling back to unmanaged startup when acquire/start fails. Extended test/tools-cli.test.mjs to assert wrapper-created browser-pool state and lease metadata. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused wrapper/browser-pool tests passed, real Docker worker wrapper validation with Chromium passed, and npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Enforce browser pool concurrency and max process limits
userStories.description: As an operator, I want concurrent browser requests to serialize or fail clearly so that one worker cannot exceed its configured browser main-process limit.
acceptanceCriteria: Pool acquire enforces the configured max browser main-process count, defaulting to one lane for compute workers
Concurrent acquire attempts cannot start duplicate browser main processes for the same pool
Busy-pool behavior is explicit: wait with timeout, queue, or fail with a pool-exhausted error according to the implemented policy
Tests simulate concurrent acquire calls and prove the browser start function is called no more than once for one-lane pools
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented one-lane browser-pool concurrency enforcement in src/tools/browser-pool.ts and the browser-use wrapper: healthy active leases for a different lease id now fail explicitly with a pool-exhausted result instead of starting or replacing another browser, same-lease reuse remains allowed, expired leases can be taken over, and wrapper default lease ids are stable per browser-use session. Added focused concurrency/busy-pool tests in test/browser-pool-state.test.mjs and wrapper busy-path coverage in test/tools-cli.test.mjs. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused browser-pool/wrapper tests passed, real Docker worker wrapper validation with Chromium proved second lease fails pool-exhausted without unmanaged fallback, and npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Expose browser pool status in text and JSON
userStories.description: As an operator, I want browser pool status output so that I can tell whether a worker browser is ready, leased, stale, dirty, or idle.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a pool status command or extend browser-use health to show pool id, state, pid, CDP URL, user-data dir, active lease id, last-used time, idle expiry, and stale/dirty reason
Text output includes concise next commands for stale or dirty states
JSON output exposes stable fields for agents and monitoring
Default status is read-only and does not start or kill browsers
CLI tests cover text output, JSON output, empty pool, ready pool, and stale pool
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented read-only `pibo tools browser-use pool status [--json]` with stable fields, text next-command hints, CLI tests, Docker real-path read-only validation, and typecheck evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md.
